description: A trait representing an asynchronous computation that may not have completed yet. Futures are the foundation of Rust's async/await system. When you write an async fn, the compiler transforms it into a state machine that implements Future. You rarely call poll() directly; instead, use .await to drive a Future to completion. Python equivalent: asyncio coroutines (awaitable objects).

methods.name: poll
methods.signature: fn poll(self: Pin<&mut Self>, cx: &mut Context<'_>) -> Poll<Self::Output>
methods.description: Attempts to resolve the future to its final value. Returns Poll::Ready(value) if done, or Poll::Pending if still waiting. You almost never call this directly; use .await instead.

methods.name: await
methods.description: Syntax for waiting on a Future to complete. Suspends the current async function until the future resolves. Can only be used inside async functions or blocks. Python equivalent: await.

methods.name: async fn
methods.description: Declares an asynchronous function. The compiler transforms it into a function that returns impl Future<Output = T>. Python equivalent: async def.

methods.name: async block
methods.description: Creates an anonymous future from a block of code. Useful for creating futures inline without defining a separate async function.

methods.name: Output
methods.description: The associated type representing the value produced when the Future completes. For async fn foo() -> i32, the Output type is i32.
